sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value1 DECIMAL(10, 5), value2 DECIMAL(10, 5), date_col DATE, str_col VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value1, value2, date_col, str_col) VALUES (1, 1.5, 3.2, '2023-10-01', 'sample1'), (2, NULL, 4.5, '2023-10-02', 'sample2'), (3, 7.8, '3.1', '2023-10-03', NULL);
SELECT id, sql_min(value1, sql_min(value2, 5.5)) AS min_value FROM test_data WHERE sql_min(value1, value2) >= 2.0 ORDER BY min_value DESC;
SELECT id, str_col FROM test_data WHERE value1 >= sql_min(CAST(str_col AS DECIMAL), 3.0) AND date_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, sql_min(value1, (SELECT MAX(value2) FROM test_data WHERE value2 IS NOT NULL)) AS min_value FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING min_value >= 6;
SELECT t1.id, sql_min(t1.value1, t2.value2) AS minimum_value FROM test_data t1 JOIN test_data t2 ON t1.id >= t2.id WHERE t1.value1 IS NOT NULL ORDER BY minimum_value;
SELECT AVG(sql_min(value1, value2)) AS average_min_value FROM test_data WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL OR value2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, sql_min(value1, sql_min(COALESCE(value2, 0), 3.8)) AS adjusted_min FROM test_data WHERE adjusted_min >= 2.5;
SELECT sql_min(NULL, value1) AS min_with_null FROM test_data WHERE date_col = '2023-10-01';
SELECT id, sql_min(value1, (SELECT sql_min(value2) FROM test_data WHERE value1 IS NULL)) AS min_value FROM test_data WHERE sql_min(value2, value1) >= 2.0 GROUP BY id;
